place: Corinth village, New York, New York

facts.text: Corinth Village was founded in 1886 after a mill and other businesses turned the area into a thriving community.
Palmer Falls, the village’s famous whitewater, was once called "Kah-che-bon-cook" and powered early saw and grist mills.
The first permanent settlement appeared in 1777 when Joseph Eggleston escaped British raiders to Jessup's Landing, now the village beach.
A shirt factory opened in the 1890s and was bought by Cluett Peabody & Company in 1899, closing only in 1975.
The town sits on the northeastern border of Saratoga County, north of Saratoga Springs, and is known as the snowshoe capital of the world.
Early settlers were drawn by the Hudson River’s waterfalls, which provided water power for mills and later shaped the village’s industrial heritage.
